11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
25	--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
26	DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
27	within the requested power bounds.
28	
29	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
30	  drained     — no ready beads remain
31	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
32	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
33	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
34	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
35	
36	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
37	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
38	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
39	`,
40			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
41	  ddx work
42	
43	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
44	  ddx work --once
45	
46	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
47	  ddx work --poll-interval 30s
48	
49	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
50	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
51	
52	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
53	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
54			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
55			RunE: f.runWork,
56		}
57	
58		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
59		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
60		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
61		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
62		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
63		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
64		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
65		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
66		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
67		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
68		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
69		cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 30*time.Second, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits (legacy opt-out). Default 30s keeps the worker alive across empty polls.")
70		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
71		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
72		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
73		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review")
74		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
76		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
77		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
78		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
79		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
80		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
81		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
82	
83		return cmd
84	}
85	
86	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
87	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
88	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
89	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
90	// by this layer.
91	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
92		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true)
93	}

1512	target project's execution-ready bead queue, claims the next ready bead,
1513	runs "ddx agent execute-bead" on it from the project root, records the
1514	structured result, and continues until no unattempted ready work remains.
1515	
1516	Reach for execute-loop by default. Use "ddx agent execute-bead" directly
1517	only as the primitive for debugging or re-running one specific bead.
1518	
1519	Planning and document-only beads are valid execution targets — any bead
1520	with unmet acceptance criteria and no blocking deps is eligible.
1521	
1522	Close semantics (per execute-bead result status):
1523	  success                      — close bead with session + commit evidence
1524	  already_satisfied            — close bead (after repeated no_changes)
1525	  no_changes                   — unclaim; may cooldown or close after retries
1526	  land_conflict                — unclaim; result preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/
1527	  post_run_check_failed        — unclaim; result preserved
1528	  execution_failed             — unclaim
1529	  structural_validation_failed — unclaim
1530	
1531	Only success (and already_satisfied) closes the bead. Every other status
1532	leaves the bead open and unclaimed so a later attempt can try again. Each
1533	attempt is appended to the bead as an execute-bead event (status, detail,
1534	base_rev, result_rev, preserve_ref, retry_after), and the underlying agent
1535	session log is recorded under the execute-bead agent-log path.

1537	execute-loop runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no
1538	separate "submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but
1539	ignored (deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.

1544	  (default)           the git root of the current working directory
1545	
1546	When submitting to a multi-project server you must ensure the target project
1547	is registered with the server (run "ddx server" from that directory, or use
1548	"ddx server projects register"). The server rejects unrecognised project paths.
1549	`,

47		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send

54	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal

56	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3

58	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
59	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
60	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
61	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
62	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
63	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
64	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
65	
66	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
67	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
68	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
69	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
70	// advance the base branch.
71	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
72	
73	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
74	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
75	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
76	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
77	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
78	// worker.
79	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
80	
81	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
82	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
83	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
84	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
85	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
86	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
87	//
88	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
89	// the cancellation immediately.
90	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
91		if ctx.Err() != nil {
92			return false
93		}
94		if runtime.Log != nil {
95			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
96		}
97		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
98			Kind:      "loop-error",
99			Summary:   operation + " failed",
100			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
101			Actor:     assignee,
102			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
103			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
104		})
105		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
106		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
107		result.Failures++
108		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
109		return true
110	}

112	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
113	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
114	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
115	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
116		if d <= 0 {
117			return 0
118		}
119		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
120			return MaxLoopCooldown
121		}
122		return d
123	}

238		Reopen(id, reason, notes string) error
239		// Update mutates a bead in place. Used by the post-merge triage step to
240		// add labels (e.g. "needs_human") and metadata hints (e.g. tier-pin) when
241		// the triage policy escalates after repeated review BLOCKs.
242		Update(id string, mutate func(*bead.Bead)) error
243	}

265	// The field names and types are identical — the server package deserialises
266	// these directly from the channel.

20	// TestMain clears all GIT_* environment variables before running tests so
21	// that tests invoking git commands operate on their own temp-dir repositories
22	// rather than any repository inherited from a hook invocation (e.g. GIT_DIR
23	// set by lefthook during pre-commit). Prefix scrub is important — lefthook
24	// sets GIT_AUTHOR_NAME, GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL, GIT_CONFIG_PARAMETERS etc. that
25	// also leak into test subprocesses and corrupt the shared repo config.
26	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
27		for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
30					_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
31				}
32			}
33		}
34		os.Exit(m.Run())
35	}

123	// syncDirectory copies files from src to dst, preserving directory structure
124	func syncDirectory(src, dst string) error {
125		return filepath.Walk(src, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
126			if err != nil {
127				return err
128			}
129	
130			// Skip .git directories from source
131			if info.IsDir() && info.Name() == ".git" {
132				return filepath.SkipDir
133			}
134	
135			// Calculate relative path
136			relPath, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
137			if err != nil {
138				return err
139			}
140	
141			dstPath := filepath.Join(dst, relPath)
142	
143			if info.IsDir() {
144				return os.MkdirAll(dstPath, info.Mode())
145			}
146	
147			// Copy file
148			srcFile, err := os.Open(path)
149			if err != nil {
150				return err
151			}
152			defer func() { _ = srcFile.Close() }()
153	
154			dstFile, err := os.Create(dstPath)
155			if err != nil {
156				return err
157			}
158			defer func() { _ = dstFile.Close() }()
159	
160			if _, err := dstFile.ReadFrom(srcFile); err != nil {
161				return err
162			}
163	
164			return os.Chmod(dstPath, info.Mode())
165		})
166	}

343			}

355			gitCommit.Dir = te.Dir
356			gitCommit.Env = cleanEnv

takes a tryTargetBeadID parameter that forces --once and dispatches
through formatTryResult for try-specific output and exit-code mapping.

data.text:
Commit landed at `5ce92857`. Lefthook reported all checks ✔️ (the cmd-package install-test failures shown are pre-existing — they require a built `cli/build/ddx` binary and fail identically on baseline, as I verified with `git stash -u`; the lefthook script's exit code reflects only the last package iterated, which passes).

Summary of changes:
- **`cli/cmd/try.go`** — new `ddx try <bead-id>` command with pre-flight gates (not found, not claimable, unmet deps), full passthrough flag surface mirroring `ddx work`, and try-specific output/exit-code mapping (0 success/already_satisfied, 1 preserved/no_changes, 2 execution_failed).
- **`cli/cmd/try_test.go`** — covers AC #2/#3/#4/#5/#6/#7: not-found, closed, cancelled, unmet-deps, flag plumbing, registration, runtime-layer happy-path with target filtering, target-not-in-ready-set, and direct preflight unit tests.
- **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`** — `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.TargetBeadID` field; `nextCandidate` filters to that bead when set.
- **`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`** — `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl` takes `tryTargetBeadID`, forces `once=true` and wires `TargetBeadID`; on try mode delegates to `formatTryResult` for output + exit-code mapping.
- **`cli/cmd/work.go`** + **`cli/cmd/command_factory.go`** — pass empty target through and register the new command.
